item.title: what the hell did i just read
item.content:encoded: This is an incredibly wacky shitpost, but most of it isn't in a style I enjoyed. The humor and dialogue in particular were mostly a miss for me. There were several boring parts that I had to skim through, but I enjoyed some of it. Overall, I feel like I've been hit with a mind-warping cognitohazard. -1.

item.title: Provides Much Needed Laughs
item.content:encoded:
Given the state that the world is in, this SCP provided some much needed medication in the form of some good laughs.

From the description of Old Gorman ("not the broom! Anything but the broom!"), the dialogue leading up to the election, to Foundation personnel finding that don't quite have political immunity following the Comey incident and Russia's suspected involvement, to Supreme Motherfucking Sorceress Extraordinaire Notorious Justice Ruth Bade Ginsberg kicking said Gorman's ass, this was hilarious.

There were one or two parts that were a bit tiring, such as the brainless dialogue from the Trumps, but for the most part, this was entertaining from start to finish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did Doctor Spanko just betray them Foundation?

For shame

Also Powerful Wizard Ruth Baden Ginsburg LMAO

Take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Too memey. I laughed at first but the joke was dragged on and on. Should be a -J.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Although this is a well-written piece of article, the SCP Foundation for me is a place to take a break from reality and indulge in fantasy. This reminds me of the tiresome political situation that we currently see. So, for me, it's a -1.

item.title: Re: Should be a -j
item.content:encoded: Why? What about this SCP means it should be a -J?

item.title: Should be a -j
item.content:encoded:
Effort or not, this should be a -j

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i really hate the idea that just because an SCP is a comedy article it has to be a -J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Political stance aside, this is very funny

The photographic material inserted in the middle causes a particularly laugh

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having politics and SCPs is dangerous

I see we're starting off with a bold claim.

this just got an higher than average rating because it was making fun of Donald Trump

Boiling down all the love this SCP has to "it was making fun of Donald Trump" ignores all the other comments in this thread.

Personally, I upvoted this because kaktus is a comedic genius.

The strangest part of this comment is:

any Political SCPs that are not joke SCPs […] don’t make it because 99/100 times it’s just trying to make fun of someone.

Let's take a look at some political (and successful) SCPs:

SCP-5092 (+94) - MTFs storm The White House to make Donald Trump scratch his nose. In the process it discusses Donald Trump's reaction to the recent civil rights protests, his narcissism, and COVID-19

SCP-5821 (+85) - Literally an SCP about people trying to eat Jeff Bezos. More specifically, it discusses wealth inequality and class interest.

SCP-4182 (+387) - A story about genocide denial.

SCP-1171 (+1614) - It's a story about racism.

SCP-4051 (+228) - This whole canon is about institutional abuse and gaslighting.

Also literally everything else because this entire wiki is about an intergovernmental, secretive, extralegal organisation that interferes with global politics and commits daily human rights abuses without oversight.

item.title: Re: what the fuck have you brought upon this cursed land
item.content:encoded: disregard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This aversion to politics in fiction is very strange to me.

1984 is one the greatest pieces of fiction ever written. It is also one of the most political pieces of fiction ever written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based on the above -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having politics and SCPs is dangerous and this was not really the greatest of all SCPs. First of all WAY to many words with everything sort of just being jumbled up together and some things not necessarily making a lick of sense. I think we need to go to the Politics part, it’s clear that there was some bias to against SCP-B because this is basically making a joke out of Trump 85% of the time. I would of maybe liked it more if it represented Trump and his family better to be more realistic but this was just a joke. There’s a clear agenda against Trump and I’d be OK with this if hit was an -J SCP but it’s not. Jokes like this don’t deserve to be part of the official foundation database and I think a lot of us know this just got an higher than average rating because it was making fun of Donald Trump. Overall any Political SCPs that are not joke SCPs whether it’s about Joe Biden, RBG(Rip), Donald Trump, or Hillary Clinton don’t make it because 99/100 times it’s just trying to make fun of someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: RIP RBG, the best Supreme Court Wizard to have ever lived.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fortunately, no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Indeed.

Plus, many pieces of fiction and literature throughout centuries have a political subtext (more or less subtle, depends on the execution). It's no different here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP is about a shadowy non governmental organization not under the jurisdiction of any authority that frequently takes morally grey actions for the Greater Good. It's all political.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you keep politics out of scp?

item.title: what the fuck have you brought upon this cursed land
item.content:encoded:
Kaktus, may I ask on what amount of whatever you took does one create such an article?

Jokes aside, this is beautiful. It's really well-written despite being a comedic piece, which is a staple of all of your works - even if I'm not the biggest fan of something1 I can't take that away from you. I didn't think you could make me laugh so hard, but you did. I swear to god, if you make a 6k entry about Vermin Supreme or someone equally beautiful I will scream of happiness.

Godspeed, you magnificent bastard.

Edit - he’s actually fucking doing it, and it’s about Vermin Supreme aaaaaaa

Footnotes
1. Though, in this case, I am.

item.title: Re-read this follwing the recent news
item.content:encoded:
I loved this SCP when I first read it and I still love it now. I didn't really know much about RBG at the time but this article inspired me to find out more and I gained a real respect for her.

Her passing is a huge loss for the world as a whole but at least we have this SCP to tickle our funny bones at such a grim time in human history.

item.title: Re-reviewed after much time.
item.content:encoded:
A while ago I wrote a really weird and kind of bad review of this SCP, and I've been reminded of it due to recent events, so I decided to revisit this and reread it and finally rereview it.

Looking back, all that I really got from this was the laughter from my initial readthrough. After that, it was just… meh? It's a giant absurd shitpost. When I first looked it over I laughed my ass off because it was shocking and nonsensical, and now I look at it and it's just… nonsense.

At this point in time, I feel that this article has nothing to offer me but far too many words and some decent image edits, and I once again change my vote on this article to a -1.

No ill will to ya kaktus. Like you've said, you can't win em all, and I've loved a lot of your other stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rest in peace, Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.

You truly were the greatest wizard of all time. The real and anomalous worlds will both miss you truly.

item.title: I can't stop laughing.
item.content:encoded: I was a taken a bit back to see Trump when I loaded up this SCS, but my god. It was so worth pushing through. I feel like this SCP is a gigantic in universe shitpost that reality itself made against the Foundation. Specifically the bits where they were going to do something, only for it to happen anyways before they could lift a finger.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Hey, you can't win em all.

Far, far too memey.
At first I cringed when I saw someone had made a Trump SCP but on realising that Trump was the SCP negator i decided to give it a go. It's actually really solid most of the way through: the idea of Gorman is fairly funny and the twist being that the Foundation's efforts to rig the election were unnecessary was a neat one.
The hints that there was another group at work rigging the election and that it might have actually been the rest of the Trump family was neat but this gets ignored in favour of the Trumps acting so stupid they'd be at home in a particularly cringe worthy episode of Family Guy or something.
RGB acting up about being a wizard was funny at first but then the actual wizarding battle with her? Just awful. Stupid, unfunny and unfitting for SCP and the sudden reliance on modern slang contradicts her earlier verbose way of speaking. Felt like something from the depths of the worst parts of Tumblr.
Add on to that Dr Bright just constantly acting stupid for laughs that fail to arrive.

You really had the seed of something good but this should have ended about half way through. The joke just drags on way too long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By the way , the rating of SCP-5004 on Chinese Branch is +191.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome.
Fuck yeah

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is, all things considered, a minor complaint, but in the section with the email headings/previews, the fact that Bright's email is all one big word causes the table to break outside of the div that holds the table. This can be fixed by wrapping one of the laughters in a word-break:break-all span (presumably the longer one), like so:

 ||**Bright, Dr. Ja...**||hahahahahahahahahahahahaha...||[[span style="word-break:break-all"]]hahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahaha...[[/span]]||

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Remember, kids, people who criticize the President in any way are thoughtless NPCs with no brains! Only sheeple would even consider making fun of authority figures!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So he was probably only Bright!Bush for half of that term.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just astonished you managed to make a skip on a controversial character and make it so unexpectedly fleshed-out that it doesn't pander to any specific audience. bravo 1+

item.title: 0/0 no sans
item.content:encoded: 0/0, no sans the skeleton. Will read again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those were four of the shittiest years of my life,

Bush was a two-termer.

item.title: LOL
item.content:encoded:
Up to now, there are only Chinese version and English original version —— Translators of other languages should work harder

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OMG I like this

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Yes, that is the idea. Glad to see you are finally catching on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This shit is hilarious. I think it kind of fell apart in the end and the RBG joke was a bit forced, but the concept is interesting and the character interactions are gold. Dr. Bright specifically is portrayed very well in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the single worst thing I have ever read

+1

Gentleman, I have a daring and radical synthesis. You may need to sit down as I deliver the theory of the ages:

Orange man…

…

…

BAD!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest, I can't really get down with this. It really feels like you picked random jokes and threw them together without any sense for cohesion, which in the smaller scheme led to rapid-fire balls-to-the-wall jokery and in the larger scheme led to a muddled narrative which I had trouble caring about. There are a couple jokes which got a brief mental grin out of me (the "as a centrist" joke is the most memorable for me), but when the jokes don't stick, they really don't stick. I found the Bright stuff in this to be particularly ehh, and some of the RBG was just way too out there for me to enjoy.

All in all, this is a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems more like a -j and not a mainline SCP…also not a fan of modern politics, it's just one massive shitshow for both sides. I'm going to have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did Dr. Bright shit their pants in this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no F#$%^ing idea what I just read, but I love every single bit of it

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just f***ing hilarious, it is just priceless.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm leaving this as a no-vote for, well, the obvious reasons. Actually, this whole thing is so good-bad-good-bad that I think even the obvious reasons are kinda weird. But I do think it has bad and good points.

The Bad:

  • It is just a giant shitpost and that's a bit off-putting to see as a main article. It would certainly be a +1 as possibly the highest-effort -J I've seen.
  • Far too verbose in the logs. I think more could've been put into brief descriptions rather than the overly-narrative form we get here. I've noticed this has become a recurring thing on the wiki though, so maybe I'm just outdated in my thinking
  • I wish more time was spent delving into the Arcane stuff set up earlier rather than having it apparently as a cheap way to make Powerful Wizard RBG (which is amazing, just under-developed).

The Good:

  • I'm just amazed you managed to to tie in so much of the election. While I'm not necessarily a fan of bringing current politics into the SCP non-canon-canon, the background concept here was pretty compelling, and I definitely would like to see more on this arcane political circle in D.C.
  • It is very funny, and all the characters are really distinct. I didn't have to keep checking the names because you've given them each their own style, where most authors here just end up writing in their own internal voice. There were lots of great references and absurdities which got a good chuckle out of me.
  • I quite enjoyed the narrative spiral in the middle portion, where the Foundation finds itself unable to actually affect change in the election because, somehow, that was happening anyway.

So while this is a great article, it's also a terrible, terrible, horrible mess of one. I'll just leave it at that because, as a centrist, I believe that [DATA CORRUPTED].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best article since The Way It Ends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the greatest SCP on the site.

item.title: He is guy
item.content:encoded: Melania Trump's 3 word responses made me laugh a little too hard. Very disappointed I missed this entry while the 5k contest was still going. +1 and may the force be with RBG!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Bright: No, you deserve this one, Sophia. You all deserve this. The cows have come home to roost for Western Regional Command, I tell you what. I knew this would happen. Every night I would kneel down by my bed and pray to sweet little baby Jesus that he would deliver the same sort of misfortune upon all of you that you laid on me for four fucking years, Sophia. And guess what? Little baby Jesus was a no-show, but this Gorman fellow sure as shit has heard my prayers. Donald goddamn Trump. Hi-fucking-larious. You all are so fucked. You're so fucked. F-U-C-K-E-D. Fucked fucked fucked fucked fucked. Thank you, new demon Jesus. Thank you for this thing you've done for me. Gorman bless America, and Gorman bless the SCP Foundation. Hallelujah, I'm coming home. Donald Trump. Fuck my bright red ass.

Lmao, this still has me dying. I love this article so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will say it again and I will say it a million more times.

What the fuck Bright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't feel like a mainlister. I don't like all the references to inside jokes, American politics (featuring Trump) and pop culture - and believe me, I'm an American that wants Trump behind bars, but I don't come to the SCP wiki for heavy-handed, not-even-trying-to-be-subtle political humor. It's reminiscent of a late-night talk show, as someone else mentioned. Occasionally funny, but often trying too hard.

Now if it were just political references, I might go for a novote, but it doesn't end with politics. There's the golden shower. Navarro saying "Nani". RBG saying "eat my ass". It feels like cheap and dirty humor going more for the shock value or unexpected pop culture reference than anything else. In short: major parts of this feel like a shitpost, not a mainlister SCP. -1

Also, a typo:

SCP-5004-A rears back and away from it, but it seemingly rooted to the ground

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not well acquainted enough to American politics to get all the references, but this is funny enough on its own (well, maybe including 4444) for me to love it. covfefe meaning [DATA EXPUNGED] was the last straw.
+1, of course.

item.title: why?
item.content:encoded:
Why and how the fuck did you make 4444 more crack born.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed but I have no idea if this is even remotely tasteful

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, man. I'm a Bernie guy and I hate Trump but I really, really hate this kind of SNL lolrandom writing. Like someone said upthread, Ginsburg is intelligent and subtle in her humor. She's never gonna say "eat my ass".

-1
